options.label: Shrouded Spirit
options.description:
The pall of death drapes over you like the Shroud of Druskenvald. This shroud protects you from the depredations of the Undead, but it draws you ever nearer to their cold embrace.

Inured to Undeath. You have Resistance to Necrotic damage, and you have Advantage on saving throws against spells and effects created by Undead.

Unholy Appetite. You must consume one pound of raw flesh (it needn’t be fresh) per day or suffer Malnutrition.

Withering Soul. Features that detect your creature type, such as a Paladin’s Divine Sense or the Detect Evil and Good spell, treat you as Undead if your creature type isn’t Undead already.

options.label: Skin of Coffin Nails
options.description:
Your body becomes as coldly unrelenting as iron nails sealing away the dead. You can shrug off staggering punishment, but the waters of the world no longer embrace you, as if spiting your defiance.

Iron Grit. You gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage (your choice when you select this Dark Bargain). You can change the damage type to one of the other options when you finish a Short or Long Rest. When you suffer a Critical Hit from the chosen damage type, it becomes a normal hit instead.

Watery Doom. When you are immersed in water or splashed with at least 1 gallon of water (such as from a bucket or being rained on), your skin tightens and grows rusty until you finish a Short or Long Rest. During that time, your Speed and the time you can hold your breath are halved, and your Resistance to the chosen damage type for Iron Grit becomes Vulnerability instead. At the GM’s discretion, other liquids can also trigger this feature.

options.label: Stilled Hungers
options.description:
Food, drink, and maladies of the flesh have lost their hold over you, but these are the very things that keep a soul vital while in the land of the living. Your mind grows ravenous absent those urges—your thoughts crack with gnawing fears, and your dreams wither into crooked nightmares.

Sated Cravings. Forgoing food and drink doesn’t give you Exhaustion levels. You have Resistance to Poison damage, and you have Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.

Tenuous Psyche. You have Vulnerability to Psychic damage and Disadvantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition. A Calm Emotions spell cast on you suppresses this feature for its duration.

options.label: Unquenchable Fury
options.description:
Anger is a powerful tool. This bargain stokes it into a formidable inferno, but it threatens to consume everything in its path.

Furious Onslaught. As a Bonus Action, or when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can choose to enter a fury. While in this fury, you can make one additional melee attack when you take the Attack action on your turn, and you reduce all damage you take by an amount equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The fury ends if you have the Incapacitated condition, if you die, or if you end your turn and there are no creatures within 60 feet of you that you can see or hear. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You also regain the use of this feature when you kill a Humanoid.

Red Haze. While you are in a fury, you must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw at the start of each of your turns. On a successful save, you can choose to end your fury or continue it. On a failed save, you no longer make the saving throw at the start of your turns, and you regard the creature nearest to you that you can see or hear as your enemy (if there are multiple, choose one at random). On each of your turns, you must move as close to the creature as possible and take the Attack action, targeting that creature with melee attacks. If you’re unable to get close enough to the creature to attack it, your turn ends after you’ve used up all your available movement. If the creature dies or can no longer be seen or heard by you, the next nearest creature that you can see or hear becomes your new target.

options.label: Wings of Whispers
options.description:
You have been gifted with secrets that you can call upon in the form of ravens and crows to bear you aloft, but at the risk of revealing your own secrets in turn.

Murderous Flight. As a Bonus Action, you can magically gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed (and you can hover) for 10 minutes. During this time, a mass of shadows takes the shape of a murder of crows and ravens (but they aren’t truly creatures). The birds appear in a 20-foot Emanation originating from you and take flight and whirl around you when you fly. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you gain 1 Exhaustion level to use it again early.

Whispered Secrets. The birds that appear when you activate Murderous Flight remain and continue to follow you until you finish a Short or Long Rest. While the birds are present, you have Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks, and the birds whisper your secrets into the minds of other creatures, granting them Advantage on Wisdom (Insight or Perception) checks made against you.

options.label: Increase three scores (+1 / +1 / +1)
options.description: Increase your Dexterity, Constitution, and Intelligence by 1.

options.label: Increase two scores (+2 / +1)
options.description: Increase one of these scores by 2 and a different score by 1.

options.label: Bloodsworn (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Cunning. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Deception, Insight, or Intimidation.

Tool of Vengeance. You gain a Common magic item of your choice.

You have been wronged. Now your fate is painted with blood.

Among the Northlanders, no oath is more serious than an oath of vengeance. But when justice cannot be achieved by any other means, a sworn promise to mete out revenge for wrongdoing is the only answer that remains. Those who make such oaths are called Bloodsworn, and their vengeance becomes a tale told by skalds for generations.

As a Bloodsworn, your ultimate goal is revenge upon the one (or ones) who wronged you. Unfortunately, the wrongdoer is beyond your reach, at least for now. This means your plan must be carried out over weeks, months, or even longer. During this time, you’ll no doubt be distracted by other adventures, but your focus demands that you always return to your oath. Bloodsworn must have been wronged or harmed in some way before the gods will accept their blood oath. In most cases, your vengeance will be fatal to your target, but in some situations, other punishments may be more fitting, especially if the wrong you’re avenging doesn’t itself involve a death.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Bloodsworn Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Bloodsworn Goals
d6 Goal
1 I must kill the one who murdered the one I love.
2 I cannot rest until the usurper who killed my jarl is dead by my hands.
3 A crew of scheming charlatans ruined my family’s business. I vow to punish them in ways the law cannot.
4 The power-mad jarl of our lands has destroyed my community. I have a cunning plan to ensure he is overthrown.
5 My trusted shieldmate betrayed me and left me to die. Now I shall return the favor.
6 I was shamed by the false words of another. I will deliver vengeance tenfold upon my accuser.

options.label: Bloodsworn (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Enemy of My Enemy. You’ve made an ally, a person who is also sworn to oppose your enemy. They have granted you an underground hideout from which to conduct your campaign of vengeance.

Wrath. You can cast Wrathful Smite, using your choice of INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting ability score. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Cunning. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Deception, Insight, or Intimidation.

Tool of Vengeance. You gain a Common magic item of your choice.

options.label: Bloodsworn (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Tenacity. You can resist effects that would slow your oath from being carried out. If you begin your turn with the Frightened, Incapacitated, Paralyzed, or Stunned condition, you may choose to end one of those conditions. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

Tool of Vengeance. You gain a Common magic item of your choice.

options.label: Bloodsworn (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Dire Oath. When you complete a Long Rest, name a specific creature you swore your oath against or who stands in the way of you completing your oath. For the next 24 hours, that creature is marked by your dire oath. While the dire oath lasts, you have Advantage on all attack rolls made against that creature, and Advantage on skill checks made to locate or learn about that creature.

After you use this feature, you must wait 7 days before you can use it again.

Wrath. You can cast Wrathful Smite, using your choice of INT, WIS, or CHA as your spellcasting ability score. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

Cunning. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Deception, Insight, or Intimidation.

Tool of Vengeance. You gain a Common magic item of your choice.

options.label: Cursemarked (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Tides of Fate [1–3, Saving Throws]. Your Speed is reduced by 10 feet. When this occurs, the Speed of another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you increases by 10 feet. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Tides of Fate is triggered, you can choose to reduce your Speed to 0 until the start of your next turn. If you do so, in addition to the normal benefit, the chosen creature does not provoke opportunity attacks and ignores Difficult Terrain until the end of your next turn. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

You bear the brunt of a curse of ill luck, and it’ll get worse before the end.

Northlanders place great faith in the existence of luck. The gods can bestow good fortune upon the faithful, and honorable actions can directly lead to successful endeavors. Likewise, disreputable behavior can bring failure not only to you, but to future generations of your family.

You are one of the unfortunates, chosen by fate or the gods to suffer from ill luck wherever you go. Perhaps you earned this curse through dishonor, oathbreaking, or cowardice. You might bear misfortune as a virtual heirloom, passed down to you by a disreputable ancestor. Other cursemarked are victims of witch’s curses or similar supernatural effects. Whatever the reason, you can’t seem to catch a break.

Strangely, the presence of a Cursemarked can often bring good fortune to those nearby. As long as your friends are willing to put up with your occasional mishaps, the long-term benefit might well be worthwhile. Meanwhile, however, the cursemarked searches for some way to atone for their misdeeds, real or imagined, knowing such a road will be long and will certainly grow worse before the end. Yet when the hand of fortune is turned against you, what else can you do?

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Cursemarked Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Cursemarked Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will atone for my dishonor by returning a sacred relic to a distant tomb.
2 I can only lift this curse by clearing the name of an ancestor falsely accused of murder.
3 I must prove my courage to the gods by defeating a tribe of marauding giants.
4 I will invoke Freyr’s blessing by forging peace between two warring clans.
5 To end this curse, I shall endure trials of my honor, my valor, and my selflessness.
6 I must convince Wotan himself to intervene and convince the Norns to reshape my fate.
Cursemarked Milestone Rules

Cursemarked milestones work a bit differently than those of other character threads. The milestone features described below trigger when you roll for specific mechanics (saving throws, ability checks or attack roll, depending on the feature) and get a result within the range of numbers indicated in the bracket for each milestone. Milestone features never overlap, so if you trigger a feature, you can’t trigger another until the start of your next turn after the duration of the previous one expires.

options.label: Cursemarked (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you lose all prior milestone feature benefits from this thread and gain the following benefits.

Burden’s Shield [1–5, Saving Throws, Ability Checks]. Your AC is reduced by 2. When this occurs, the AC of another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you increases by 2. These effects last until the end of your next turn.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Burden’s Shield is triggered, you can choose to reduce your AC by 4 instead of 2. If you do so, the AC of the chosen creature increases by 4 (instead of 2), or you can choose for it to receive the benefit of the Tide of Fate’s Greater Sacrifice. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Cursemarked (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you lose all prior milestone feature benefits from this thread and gain the following benefits.

Threads Entwined [1–7, Saving Throws, Ability Checks, Attack Rolls]. You can’t take Bonus Actions or Reactions until the end of your next turn. When this occurs, another creature of your choice within 30 feet of you can immediately use its Reaction to take the Dash, Help, or Hide action, or make a single weapon attack against a target in range.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Threads Entwined is triggered, you can instead choose to do nothing this turn besides move. If you’ve already taken your action this turn, you instead forgo your action on your next turn. If you do so, the chosen creature can take one additional action on its next turn, except the Magic action, or it can receive the benefit of one of the previous milestone feature’s Greater Sacrifices. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you lose all prior milestone feature benefits from this thread and gain the following benefits.

Two-Edged Gift [1–9, Saving Throws, Ability Checks, Attack Rolls]. Your weapon attacks and spells deal half their normal damage until the end of your next turn. During this time, the first time a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you deals damage with a weapon or spell, that creature deals maximum damage.

Greater Sacrifice. When your Two-Edged Gift is triggered, you can instead choose deal no damage with weapons and spells until the end of your next turn. If you do so, the next weapon or spell attack the chosen creature makes before the end of its next turn is automatically a Critical Hit, or it can receive the benefit of one of the previous milestone feature’s Greater Sacrifice. Once you use your Greater Sacrifice feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Explorer (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

Glory lies just over the horizon. Time to find it.

Explorers occupy an unusual place in Northlands society. Though revered for their courage, they rarely find a comfortable place in a community. Driven by wanderlust, curiosity, boredom, or a simple inability to relate to civilized folks, the Explorer spends most of their time in the wilderness. Some climb mountains, others set sail for lost lands, and still others delve through dark tunnels beneath the earth. For an Explorer, the greatest treasure awaits beyond the next hill.

Whether by choice or by fate, you follow the path less traveled. Perhaps you’re a rugged seafarer or mountaineer seeking discoveries in the wild. Other Explorers are treasure hunters, royal mapmakers, or seekers of truth. You might be an exile, outlaw, or iconoclast, who simply can’t get along with others. Or you might be driven by vision or prophecy to forsake hearth and home for the unknown.

 Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Explorer Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Rangers in the party. Consult with your Ranger players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Explorer Goals
d6 Goal
1 I shall find a route through the Bloodfjord Mountains never before traveled by mortals.
2 To avert Ragnarok, I must carry a prophecy to a far-flung land.
3 My mother died searching for the headwaters of the Tearstain River. I shall complete her quest.
4 I seek a sapling of the World Tree, rumored to be growing on a tiny island in the Nieder Strait.
5 Once I decipher the ancient map I hold, I’ll delve into the depths of Narvegr Maw.
6 The Frozen Palace of the last Hyperborean lies somewhere in the Bleak Expanse. I’ll find it and return with his icy crown.

options.label: Explorer (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Traversal Expert. You have a knack for finding easy paths through the environment. You may use your Bonus Action to move your Speed. During this movement, you ignore Difficult Terrain and have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to climb, jump, or swim. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Scout’s Awareness. You have a preternatural sense that allows you to avoid dangers in your surroundings. For 1 hour, you and each ally within 30 feet who can see you have Advantage on saving throws to resist the effects of nonmagical traps and hazards. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

options.label: Explorer (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Ennobled. A monarch has recognized your achievements as an Explorer in their or other territories by granting you a noble title, which comes with a moderately sized estate. You have Advantage on Charisma checks with anyone who would be impressed or intimidated by your title. You also can avoid punishment for minor infractions, though if you abuse this, your title might be revoked.

Wayfarer’s Steps. You can never become lost by nonmagical means and can always retrace your steps. Additionally, as a Bonus Action, you can take the Dash action and command up to 4 willing creatures who can hear you to use their Reaction to immediately take a Dash action. Neither you nor your allies trigger Opportunity Attacks while moving in this way. Once you use this Bonus Action, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Traversal Expert. You have a knack for finding easy paths through the environment. You may use your Bonus Action to move your Speed. During this movement, you ignore Difficult Terrain and have Advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks to climb, jump, or swim. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Scout’s Awareness. You have a preternatural sense that allows you to avoid dangers in your surroundings. For 1 hour, you and each ally within 30 feet who can see you have Advantage on saving throws to resist the effects of nonmagical traps and hazards. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Celebrity Explorer. You receive a 30% discount on lodging, supplies, and food from innkeepers, merchants, and proprietors, and it’s common for them to offer you things for free.

Explorer’s Aptitude. Your travels instill you with new knowledge. You gain proficiency in your choice of one of the following skills: Athletics, History, or Perception.

options.label: Explorer (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Far Traveler. While traveling, you and your companions may travel at a Fast Pace without any penalties. You always find good campsites and have Advantage on any d20 rolls related to camping or foraging in the wilderness. You also have Advantage on Animal Handling checks to calm wild animals.

One With the Land. You may study the land from a good vantage point (such as a high position or advantageous overlook) for 1 minute and ask a question concerning the natural world or locations within 5 miles. This functions like the Commune with Nature spell. Once you use this feature, you must wait 24 hours before using it again.

Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Ennobled. A monarch has recognized your achievements as an Explorer in their or other territories by granting you a noble title, which comes with a moderately sized estate. You have Advantage on Charisma checks with anyone who would be impressed or intimidated by your title. You also can avoid punishment for minor infractions, though if you abuse this, your title might be revoked.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

Your fate is written. Make it glorious.

Many warriors loudly boast of their desire for a noble death or an honorable end in battle, to thus earn a seat in the great hall of Valhalla. These boasts are but hopeful wishes since most individuals never see the path of their future. You, however, are not most individuals. You have been blessed, or perhaps cursed, with the knowledge of your end, though this knowledge doesn’t tell you if you succeed or fail in the circumstance surrounding your death. You might know you will die facing a dark warlord, but you don’t know if you will defeat her. Despite this, the Norns have given you a rare opportunity. The knowledge you possess will help you ensure your death is worthy of the sagas.

Some of those who are Fatebound are marked from birth. Others learn of their fate from seers, shamans, or divine visions. Some heroes earn this thread after engaging in activities that move the gods or the Norns to reward them with such a potent end.

Fatebound and Death. Keep in mind that having this thread may mean you can’t die before your fated time, but it doesn’t mean you will reach that point whole and healthy. While your fate is inevitable, luck alone determines your condition when you meet that end. Do you lead a glorious charge across the battlefield? Or are you left guarding the baggage? Has every confrontation leading up to your end prepared you to defeat your ultimate foe? Or has a lifetime of avoidance left you ill-prepared?

If a Fatebound PC is killed before their time, and magical resurrection is not an option, a GM could instead choose for them to be injured in a significant way—such as the loss of a limb or eye. These injuries should not be able to be healed without high-level magic. Alternatively, perhaps they lose one of their powerful magical items instead of their lives, or they might suffer a level of Exhaustion until they reach their next milestone. The GM and player should agree on this complication.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Fatebound Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note: The Fatebound is an unusual thread in that its final milestone specifically describes the manner in which a character might meet with a permanent death, likely as part of the final accomplishment of their goal, and then empowers the PCs’ allies as a reward. Most importantly, this thread should be something the player consciously selects.

Fatebound Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will die only after finding and facing the mightiest dragon in the land.
2 I will be slain by Utgard-Loki while attempting to thwart his plan to slay the gods.
3 To defend my people from invasion, I pledge to pay any price, including my own life.
4 I shall search for and find a fabled lost treasure, but I will die in the process.
5 I will rise to become the North’s greatest reaver, then perish battling the World Serpent.
6 I seek the truth behind the fall of Nordheim, though it will cost my life to learn it.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Strength of Wyrd. While you are Bloodied, once per turn you may add your Proficiency Bonus to a damage roll. You may use this damage bonus a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Enduring Wyrd. When you take damage, you may use your Reaction to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You cannot use this feature again until you complete a Short or Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Doom Delayed. When you would be killed, you may instead declare yourself as Stable with 0 Hit Points. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Knight of Fate. You have become a legend in a territory, and the monarch there has knighted you, granting you holdings that will revert back to the monarchy upon your death. You have Advantage on Charisma-based checks with the people of this land and are frequently showered with minor gifts and favors there.

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Last Act of Fate. At a moment you have predetermined to be the time when you are to die, when you are reduced 0 Hit Points or killed outright, you may (no action required) instead set your Hit Points to 1. You lose any detrimental conditions, and any harmful effects on you are ended immediately. Until the end of your next turn, you gain Advantage on all attack rolls and ability checks, you have Immunity to all damage and conditions (and other effects at the GM’s discretion), and you add your character level to all damage rolls.

At the end of your next turn, you die and can never be restored to life.

Glorious End. If you achieve your destiny in a way that earns you a seat in Valhalla (or some other appropriate afterlife), the allies who witness your death have Advantage on all d20 rolls for 24 hours as they are inspired by your heroism.

options.label: Herald (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Storyteller’s Knack. You learn three languages of your choice.

Performer’s Reward. If you have at least 3 days of downtime between adventures, you can earn GP equal to 10 times your character level by performing your stories in taverns, on the street, or for powerful patrons. Additional downtime events don’t increase this reward.

Your stories don’t just recount events . . . they shape history.

Storytellers are revered in the Northlands. Whether a skald sharing tales around a fire, a lore keeper gathering sagas to pass down the generations, or a grizzled reaver frightening young warriors by recalling harrowing battles, the tradition of collecting stories is respected across nearly all cultures in the region. As a Herald, you are a proud, if temporary, part of this tradition, driven by a need to spread stories during your travels to accomplish a specific end.

Perhaps your stories encourage downtrodden karls to rise up against their cruel jarl, warn a region about an approaching invasion, or convince warriors to join your cause. Part skald, part herald, part envoy, you will use your tales and stories to sway the minds of your audience and help shape the world.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Herald Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Bards in the party. Consult with your Bard players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Herald Goals
d6 Goal
1 The feared invasion from Jotunheim approaches, and the country must be warned.
2 By telling the sagas, I will gather support for the greatest raid the North has ever launched.
3 My tales flatter Thor and Wotan in the hope they will support our struggle against the forces of Chernobog and Angrboda.
4 I spread the word of the great crusade to eradicate the darkness beneath Tanserhall. Heed my call!
5 I seek a lost treasure of Hyperborea, and by spreading tales of similar treasures, I hope to find a path to a hidden vault.
6 To rouse the people against their evil king, I shall assemble an array of sorrowful tales of corruption that cannot be ignored.

options.label: Herald (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Enthralling Speaker. You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Expertise. Additionally, while performing before an audience, you can cast either Charm Person or Suggestion as part of your performance without requiring somatic or material components. Use Charisma as your spellcasting ability score. After casting one of these spells using this feature, you can’t use the feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

Storyteller’s Knack. You learn three languages of your choice.

options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Persuasive Words. If you make a Charisma check to change a creature's attitude or convince them of your cause, you may treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier per Long Rest.

options.label: Herald (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Heroic Saga. Your tales grow so powerful that they can briefly tamper with luck. After you spend at least 10 minutes telling an inspiring story or legend, choose either one creature who can hear you, or up to three creatures. You can’t select yourself as a target. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until for 7 days.

Depending on the number of targets, creatures can receive one of the following.

Luck Boon (Three Targets). Once before the next Long Rest, a target can expend their boon to gain Advantage on a saving throw, attack roll, or ability check.

Wyrd Boon (One Target). Once before their next Long Rest, if the target does not succeed on a saving throw or attack roll, they can choose to succeed instead. An attack roll succeeding in this way results in the minimum needed to succeed (not a Critical Hit, for example).

Song of Matrimony. A member of the royal family in a particular land has fallen in love with you and your performances, and the feeling is mutual. You are engaged to marry into the royal family.

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

Neither storm nor night nor bloody fang shall slow your relentless pursuit.

Hunting is as old as the Northlands itself. From volcanic peaks to frigid fens and aurora-bathed glaciers, the tales of hunters are sung alongside those of heroes and gods. While some may hunt for survival or vengeance, you are different. You are a hunter to be feared, as you hunt for the thrill of the chase and the glory of victory.

As a Legend Hunter, you may have been chosen by the Norns, blessed by a god, or forged in trials of blood and grift. No matter how you come to it, your extraordinary search for greater and greater prey sets you apart from those who hunt merely for survival. You do not merely forge trails; you craft sagas. You do not merely slay beasts; you end legends to become a legend.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Legend Hunter Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Legend Hunter Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will prove my valor by tracking down and slaying a legendary monster that has been destroying villages.
2 I hunt for a mythical two-headed jotun somewhere in the Bleak Expanse.
3 Besting a great dragon in its lair shall be my ultimate victory.
4 I will go beneath the waves to bring low the mother of all krakens.
5 I will bring back the head of Fenris.
6 My legend will be assured when I go among the roots of Yggdrasil to slay Nidhogg.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Unclouded Sight. Your honed senses let you ignore obscurement caused by weather, such as rain, snow, or fog. This includes magical clouds and weather effects, such as Fog Cloud and Sleet Storm, but not Darkness.

Reliable Senses. After failing an Intelligence (Investigation), Wisdom (Perception), or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can reroll the check and take the higher result. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Legend Breaker. When you hit a creature that has Legendary Resistance or Legendary Actions, you can choose to forgo dealing damage to force that creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength or Wisdom modifier and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target becomes unable use its Legendary Resistance or its Legendary Actions. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

The Ultimate Favor. You performed a service for a powerful healer, who was caught in an animal trap set by another hunter. In return, they have offered to cast a single spell of healing of 6th to 9th level on you or a creature of your choice, at a time of your choosing, even if this puts them at great risk. This should be a spell that heals, regenerates, resurrects, or that removes conditions or curses.

Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Jarl’s Authority. The authority granted you by your loyal service can sway the opinions of others. Before rolling an Intimidation or Persuasion check to influence someone who knows and respects (or fears) your ruler, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as if you’d rolled a natural 15. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

You carry out the will of your king, no matter the risk.

You have pledged your loyalty to an important personage, promising to guard them or travel across the Northlands in their service, risking danger as you seek to accomplish a great task. In the past, it was expected that a huskarl would swear to serve a lord for life or until they were dismissed. While jarls still take huskarls in that way, they also accept the service of huskarls on a temporary basis, to perform specific deeds or until certain threats are dealt with. These are the types of huskarls described in this thread.

The sworn huskarl enjoys a position of respect within their lord’s retinue. More than a mere bodyguard, you are a trusted agent acting on your ruler’s behalf. You carry some of their authority with you, but that also comes with the responsibility to act in ways they would approve. Sworn huskarls who bring shame to their jarl don’t remain in service for long.

A jarl takes on a new huskarl with a simple ceremony in which the huskarl swears an oath in the longhouse before a gathering of other members of the felag. During the oath-swearing, the goals or timeframe for the service are mentioned (unless the jarl keeps your mission a secret). Traditionally, your reasons for serving are respect for the lord, payment, or power. However, in some cases your oath may hold a secret. Perhaps you made it under threat of violence or under false pretenses. Or maybe your lord doesn’t deserve such loyalty. You might serve under protest or with your own goal that’s unknown to your ruler.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Sworn Huskarl Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Sworn Huskarl Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will find my king’s long-lost child so that my kingdom will have a true heir.
2 My queen depends on me to find and slay the pretender who claims a blood right to her throne.
3 I must root out a hidden sect of the Cult of Ragnarok that grows within my jarl’s land.
4 Giant raiding parties threaten the land. My jarl calls upon me to destroy them and make the land safe once more.
5 I can only earn my freedom by finding the stolen crown of my sadistic monarch.
6 I serve only until I can discover a way to overthrow this corrupt king, who took the throne through dark sorcery.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Extreme Loyalty. Your dedication to your ruler protects you from the control of others.

If you end your turn with the Charmed condition, you may choose to take Psychic damage equal to your character level, then end that condition. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Jarl’s Gift. To help you carry out their will, your ruler gives you your choice of one of the following uncommon magic items (or another you and your GM agree upon): Bracers of Archery, Defender’s Honey, Sentinel Shield, +1 Wand of the War Mage, or +1 Weapon.

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Undying Loyalty. The strength of your oath steels you when the tides of battle turn against you. When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can use this feature to instead set your Hit Points to be equal to your character level. This doesn’t help you against an effect that would kill you instantly without dealing damage. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again for 7 days.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

options.label: Cloud’s Jaunt (Cloud Giant)
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

options.label: Fire’s Burn (Fire Giant)
options.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d10 Fire damage to that target.

options.label: Frost’s Chill (Frost Giant)
options.description: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can also deal 1d6 Cold damage to that target and reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

options.label: Hill’s Tumble (Hill Giant)
options.description: When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack roll and deal damage to it, you can give that target the Prone condition.

options.label: Stone’s Endurance (Stone Giant)
options.description: When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled and reduce the damage by that total.

options.label: Storm’s Thunder (Storm Giant)
options.description: When you take damage from a creature within 60 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature.

options.label: Activate Large Form
options.description: Starting at character level 5, you can change your size to Large as a Bonus Action if you’re in a big enough space. This transformation lasts for 10 minutes or until you end it (no action required). For that duration, you have Advantage on Strength checks, and your Speed increases by 10 feet. Once you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Increase two scores (+2 / +1)
options.description: Increase one scores by 2 and a different score by 1.

options.label: Increase three scores (+1 / +1 / +1)
options.description: Increase three different scores by 1.

class.name: Leave Druidic Message
class.description:
You know Druidic, the secret language of Druids. While learning this ancient tongue, you also unlocked the magic of communicating with animals; you always have the [spells]Speak with Animals[/spells] spell prepared.

You can use Druidic to leave hidden messages. You and others who know Druidic automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message’s presence with a successful DC 15 Intelligence ([skill]Investigation[/skill]) check but can’t decipher it without magic.
class.snippet: You can use Druidic to leave hidden message. You and others who know Druidic automatically spot such a message. Others spot the message’s presence with a successful DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check but can’t decipher it without magic.

class.name: Wild Shape
class.description:
The power of nature allows you to assume the form of an animal. As a Bonus Action, you [rules]shape-shifting;shape-shift[/rules] into a Beast form that you have learned for this feature (see “Known Forms” below). You stay in that form for a number of hours equal to half your Druid level or until you use Wild Shape again, have the [condition]Incapacitated[/condition] condition, or die. You can also leave the form early as a Bonus Action.

Number of Uses. You can use Wild Shape twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

You gain additional uses when you reach certain Druid levels, as shown in the Wild Shape column of the Druid Features table.

Known Forms. You know four Beast forms for this feature, chosen from among Beast stat blocks that have a maximum Challenge Rating of 1/4 and that lack a Fly Speed. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one of your known forms with another eligible form.

When you reach certain Druid levels, your number of known forms and the maximum Challenge Rating for those forms increases, as shown in the Beast Shapes table. In addition, starting at level 8, you can adopt a form that has a Fly Speed.

When choosing known forms, you may look in the Monster Manual or elsewhere for eligible Beasts if the Dungeon Master permits you to do so.

Beast Shapes
Druid Level Known Forms Max CR Fly Speed
2 4 1/4 No
4 6 1/2 No
8 8 1 Yes

Rules While Shape-Shifted. While in a form, you retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak, and the following rules apply:

Temporary Hit Points. When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain a number of [rules]Temporary Hit Points[/rules] equal to your Druid level.

Game Statistics. Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast’s stat block, but you retain your creature type; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats. You also retain your skill and saving throw proficiencies and use your Proficiency Bonus for them, in addition to gaining the proficiencies of the creature. If a skill or saving throw modifier in the Beast’s stat block is higher than yours, use the one in the stat block.

No Spellcasting. You can’t cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn’t break your [rules]Concentration[/rules] or otherwise interfere with a spell you’ve already cast.

Objects. Your ability to handle objects is determined by the form’s limbs rather than your own. In addition, you choose whether your equipment falls in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it’s practical for the new form to wear a piece of equipment based on the creature’s size and shape. Your equipment doesn’t change size or shape to match the new form, and any equipment that the new form can’t wear must either fall to the ground or merge with the form. Equipment that merges with the form has no effect while you’re in that form.
class.snippet: You shape-shift into a Beast form that you have learned for this feature. You stay in that form for {{(classlevel/2)@rounddown}} hours or until you use Wild Shape again, have the Incapacitated condition, or die. You can also leave the form early as a Bonus Action. Rules While Shape-Shifted. The following rules apply while you’re shape-shifted: • You retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak. • When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain {{classlevel#unsigned}} Temporary HP. • Your game statistics are replaced by the Beast’s stat block, but you retain your creature type; HP; HP Dice; Int., Wis., and Cha. scores; class features; languages; and feats. • You retain your skill and saving throws and use your Proficiency Bonus for them, and gain any proficiencies of the creature. If the Beast’s stat block has a higher skill or saving throw modifier, use the one in the stat block. • You can’t cast spells, but shape-shifting doesn’t break your Concentration. • Your ability to handle objects is handled by the form’s limbs rather than your own. You can also choose whether your equipment falls in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it’s practical for the new form to wear the equipment. Additionally, your equipment doesn’t change size, and any equipment that the new form can’t wear must fall to the ground or merge into the form. Any equipment that merges with the form has no effect while you’re in that form.

class.name: Wild Resurgence: Regain Wild Shape
class.description:
Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot (no action required).

In addition, you can expend one use of Wild Shape (no action required) to give yourself a level 1 spell slot, but you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
class.snippet: Once on each of your turns, if you have no uses of Wild Shape left, you can give yourself one use by expending a spell slot.

class.name: Wild Resurgence: Regain Spell Slot
class.description:

class.name: Wild Shape: Circle Forms
class.description:
You can channel lunar magic when you assume a Wild Shape form, granting you the benefits below.

Challenge Rating. The maximum Challenge Rating for the form equals your Druid level divided by 3 (round down).

Armor Class. Until you leave the form, your AC equals 13 plus your Wisdom modifier if that total is higher than the Beast’s AC.

Temporary Hit Points. You gain a number of [rules]Temporary Hit Points[/rules] equal to three times your Druid level.
class.snippet:
When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain the following benefits:
    • The max CR for the form is {{(classlevel/3)@rounddown}}.
    • Until you leave the form, your AC is {{(13+modifier:wis)#unsigned}} unless the Beast’s AC is higher.
    • You gain {{3*classlevel}} Temporary HP.

Lunar Radiance. Each of your attacks in a Wild Shape form can deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. You make this choice each time you hit with those attacks.

Increased Toughness. You can add your Wisdom modifier to your Constitution saving throws.

class.name: Initiate a Circle Spell
class.description: You take a Magic action to initiate casting a Circle spell. When you do so, choose which Circle casting option you’re using for this casting; you must also meet any of the other requirements described in that option’s text. Until the Circle spell’s casting is complete, you must maintain Concentration on the spell.
class.snippet: You take a Magic action to initiate casting a Circle spell. When you do so, choose which Circle casting option you’re using for this casting; you must also meet any of the other requirements described in that option’s text. Until the Circle spell’s casting is complete, you must maintain Concentration on the spell.

class.name: Circle Spell: Augment
class.description: When you cast a spell with a range of at least 5 feet, you can increase the range of the spell by 1,000 feet per secondary caster contributing to the spell, up to a maximum of a 1-mile increase.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell with a range of at least 5 ft., you can increase the range of the spell by 1,000 ft. per secondary caster contributing to the spell, up to a max of a 1-mile increase.

class.name: Circle Spell: Distribute
class.description: When you cast a spell that requires Concentration, you can distribute the mental load of the spell among you and the secondary casters. Once the casting is complete, you and all secondary casters can maintain Concentration on this spell. As long as at least one caster who contributed to the spell maintains this Concentration, the spell’s effects remain active.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell that requires Concentration, you can distribute the mental load of the spell among you and the secondary casters. Once the casting is complete, you and all secondary casters can maintain Concentration on this spell. As long as at least one caster who contributed to the spell maintains this Concentration, the spell’s effects remain active.

class.name: Circle Spell: Expand
class.description:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can increase one dimension of the spell’s area of effect for this casting by 10 feet per secondary caster contributing to the spell. For example, if a spell creates a 20-foot-radius Sphere, casting it as a Circle spell with two secondary casters would increase the radius to 40 feet. A spell that creates a Line that is 5 feet wide and 300 feet long, cast as a Circle spell with two secondary casters, could make a Line that is either 25 feet wide or 320 feet long.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Circle Spell: Prolong
class.description:
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can increase the duration of the spell depending on the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell, as detailed in the table below.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

Spell Duration
Secondary Casters The Spell Increases Duration by
1–3 1 hour
4–6 8 hours
7+ 24 hours
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 min or longer, you can increase the duration of the spell depending on the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Circle Spell: Safeguard
class.description:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can carve out a safe zone within that area of effect that is unaffected by the spell for its duration. This safe zone consists of a number of 5-foot Cubes equal to your spellcasting ability modifier plus the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell (minimum of one Cube). You can arrange the Cubes as you like, but each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube.

If the spell’s area of effect can be moved, the safe zone moves with it.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can carve out a safe zone within that area of effect that is unaffected by the spell for its duration. This safe zone consists of a number of 5-ft. Cubes equal to {{modifier:cha#unsigned}} + the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell (min of 1 Cube). You can arrange the Cubes as you like, but each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube. If the spell’s area of effect can be moved, the safe zone moves with it.

class.name: Circle Spell: Supplant
class.description:
When you cast a spell that requires at least one Material component with a specified cost that is consumed by the spell, you can reduce the minimum cost of one such Material component by 50 GP per each secondary caster contributing to the spell. For example, casting [spells]Revivify[/spells] as a Circle spell with two secondary casters would reduce the Material component’s minimum cost to 200 GP.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot of a level greater than or equal to the spell’s level (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Primal Strike
class.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form’s attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra 1d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).
class.snippet: Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or a Beast form’s attack in Wild Shape, you can cause the target to take an extra {{((classlevel-1)/7)@rounddown,min:1}}d8 Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (choose when you hit).

feat.name: Luck Points
feat.description:
You have a number of Luck Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus and can spend the points on the benefits below. You regain your expended Luck Points when you finish a Long Rest.

Advantage. When you roll a d20 for a D20 Test, you can spend 1 Luck Point to give yourself Advantage on the roll.

Disadvantage. When a creature rolls a d20 for an attack roll against you, you can spend 1 Luck Point to impose Disadvantage on that roll.
feat.snippet: You have {{proficiency}} Luck Points that you can spend on the benefits below. You regain expended Luck Points after a Long Rest. Advantage. When you roll a d20 for a D20 Test, you can spend 1 Luck Point to give yourself Advantage on the roll. Disadvantage. When a creature rolls a d20 for an attack roll against you, you can spend 1 Luck Point to impose Disadvantage on that roll.

feat.name: Survivor: Hypervigilance
feat.description: Whenever you roll Initiative, you can reroll the d20 if the number rolled is 9 or lower. You must use the new roll.
feat.snippet: Whenever you roll Initiative, you can reroll the d20 if the number rolled is 9 or lower. You must use the new roll.

feat.name: Survivor: Steel Yourself
feat.description:
When you fail a saving throw to avoid or end the [condition]Charmed[/condition] or [condition]Frightened[/condition] condition, you can take a Reaction to add a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus is equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

Once you take this Reaction, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
feat.snippet: Once per Long Rest, when you fail a saving throw to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can take a Reaction to add {{proficiency#signed}} to the roll.

definition.name: Produce Flame
definition.description:
A flickering flame appears in your hand and remains there for the duration. While there, the flame emits no heat and ignites nothing, and it sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The spell ends if you cast it again.

Until the spell ends, you can take a Magic action to hurl fire at a creature or an object within 60 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Fire damage.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

definition.name: Druidcraft
definition.description:
Whispering to the spirits of nature, you create one of the following effects within range.

Weather Sensor. You create a Tiny, harmless sensory effect that predicts what the weather will be at your location for the next 24 hours. The effect might manifest as a golden orb for clear skies, a cloud for rain, falling snowflakes for snow, and so on. This effect persists for 1 round.

Bloom. You instantly make a flower blossom, a seed pod open, or a leaf bud bloom.

Sensory Effect. You create a harmless sensory effect, such as falling leaves, spectral dancing fairies, a gentle breeze, the sound of an animal, or the faint odor of skunk. The effect must fit in a 5-foot Cube.

Fire Play. You light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a campfire.

definition.name: Speak with Animals
definition.description:
For the duration, you can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the Influence action’s skill options with them.

Most Beasts have little to say about topics that don’t pertain to survival or companionship, but at minimum, a Beast can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever it has perceived within the past day.

definition.name: Entangle
definition.description:
Grasping plants sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square within range. For the duration, these plants turn the ground in the area into Difficult Terrain. They disappear when the spell ends.

Each creature (other than you) in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition until the spell ends. A Restrained creature can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC. On a success, it frees itself from the grasping plants and is no longer Restrained by them.

definition.name: Detect Magic
definition.description:
For the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the Magic action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell’s school of magic.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

definition.name: Detect Poison and Disease
definition.description:
For the duration, you sense the location of poisons, poisonous or venomous creatures, and magical contagions within 30 feet of yourself. You sense the kind of poison, creature, or contagion in each case.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

definition.name: Revivify
definition.description: You touch a creature that has died within the last minute. That creature revives with 1 Hit Point. This spell can’t revive a creature that has died of old age, nor does it restore any missing body parts.
definition.componentsDescription: a diamond worth 300+ GP, which the spell consumes

modifiers.restriction: Target creature that has died within the last minute

definition.name: Plant Growth
definition.description:
This spell channels vitality into plants. The casting time you use determines whether the spell has the Overgrowth or the Enrichment effect below.

Overgrowth. Choose a point within range. All normal plants in a 100-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point become thick and overgrown. A creature moving through that area must spend 4 feet of movement for every 1 foot it moves. You can exclude one or more areas of any size within the spell’s area from being affected.

Enrichment. All plants in a half-mile radius centered on a point within range become enriched for 365 days. The plants yield twice the normal amount of food when harvested. They can benefit from only one Plant Growth per year.

definition.name: Feign Death
definition.description:
You touch a willing creature and put it into a cataleptic state that is indistinguishable from death.

For the duration, the target appears dead to outward inspection and to spells used to determine the target’s status. The target has the Blinded and Incapacitated conditions, and its Speed is 0.

The target also has Resistance to all damage except Psychic damage, and it has Immunity to the Poisoned condition.

definition.name: Wind Wall
definition.description:
A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose so long as it makes one continuous path along the ground. The wall lasts for the duration.

When the wall appears, each creature in its area makes a Strength saving throw, taking 4d8 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

The strong wind keeps fog, smoke, and other gases at bay. Small or smaller flying creatures or objects can’t pass through the wall. Loose, lightweight materials brought into the wall fly upward. Arrows, bolts, and other ordinary projectiles launched at targets behind the wall are deflected upward and miss automatically. Boulders hurled by Giants or siege engines, and similar projectiles, are unaffected. Creatures in gaseous form can’t pass through it.

definition.name: Locate Creature
definition.description:
Describe or name a creature that is familiar to you. You sense the direction to the creature’s location if that creature is within 1,000 feet of you. If the creature is moving, you know the direction of its movement.

The spell can locate a specific creature known to you or the nearest creature of a specific kind (such as a human or a unicorn) if you have seen such a creature up close—within 30 feet—at least once. If the creature you described or named is in a different form, such as under the effects of a Flesh to Stone or Polymorph spell, this spell doesn’t locate the creature.

This spell can’t locate a creature if any thickness of lead blocks a direct path between you and the creature.

definition.name: Hallucinatory Terrain
definition.description:
You make natural terrain in a 150-foot Cube in range look, sound, and smell like another sort of natural terrain. Thus, open fields or a road can be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other difficult or impassable terrain. A pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road. Manufactured structures, equipment, and creatures within the area aren’t changed.

The tactile characteristics of the terrain are unchanged, so creatures entering the area are likely to notice the illusion. If the difference isn’t obvious by touch, a creature examining the illusion can take the Study action to make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to disbelieve it. If a creature discerns that the terrain is illusory, the creature sees a vague image superimposed on the real terrain.

definition.name: Giant Insect
definition.description:
You summon a giant centipede, spider, or wasp (chosen when you cast the spell). It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range and uses the Giant Insect stat block. The form you choose determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don’t issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Use the spell slot’s level for the spell’s level in the stat block.

Giant Insect

Large Beast, Unaligned

AC 11 + the spell’s level

HP 30 + 10 for each spell level above 4

Speed 40 ft., Climb 40 ft., Fly 40 ft. (Wasp only)

    Mod Save
STR 17 +3 +3
DEX 13 +1 +1
CON 15 +2 +2
    Mod Save
INT 4 −3 −3
WIS 14 +2 +2
CHA 3 −4 −4

Senses Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 12

Languages Understands the languages you know

CR None (XP 0; PB equals your Proficiency Bonus)

Traits

Spider Climb. The insect can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Actions

Multiattack. The insect makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell’s level (round down).

Poison Jab. Melee Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, reach 10 ft. Hit: 1d6 + 3 plus the spell’s level Piercing damage plus 1d4 Poison damage.

Web Bolt (Spider Only). Ranged Attack Roll: Bonus equals your spell attack modifier, range 60 ft. Hit: 1d10 + 3 plus the spell’s level Bludgeoning damage, and the target’s Speed is reduced to 0 until the start of the insect’s next turn.

Bonus Actions

Venomous Spew (Centipede Only). Constitution Saving Throw: Your spell save DC, one creature the insect can see within 10 feet. Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the insect’s next turn.

definition.name: Control Water
definition.description:
Until the spell ends, you control any water inside an area you choose that is a Cube up to 100 feet on a side, using one of the following effects. As a Magic action on your later turns, you can repeat the same effect or choose a different one.

Flood. You cause the water level of all standing water in the area to rise by as much as 20 feet. If you choose an area in a large body of water, you instead create a 20-foot tall wave that travels from one side of the area to the other and then crashes. Any Huge or smaller vehicles in the wave’s path are carried with it to the other side. Any Huge or smaller vehicles struck by the wave have a 25 percent chance of capsizing.

The water level remains elevated until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. If this effect produced a wave, the wave repeats on the start of your next turn while the flood effect lasts.

Part Water. You part water in the area and create a trench. The trench extends across the spell’s area, and the separated water forms a wall to either side. The trench remains until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. The water then slowly fills in the trench over the course of the next round until the normal water level is restored.

Redirect Flow. You cause flowing water in the area to move in a direction you choose, even if the water has to flow over obstacles, up walls, or in other unlikely directions. The water in the area moves as you direct it, but once it moves beyond the spell’s area, it resumes its flow based on the terrain. The water continues to move in the direction you chose until the spell ends or you choose a different effect.

Whirlpool. You cause a whirlpool to form in the center of the area, which must be at least 50 feet square and 25 feet deep. The whirlpool lasts until you choose a different effect or the spell ends. The whirlpool is 5 feet wide at the base, up to 50 feet wide at the top, and 25 feet tall. Any creature in the water and within 25 feet of the whirlpool is pulled 10 feet toward it. When a creature enters the whirlpool for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d8 Bludgeoning damage. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage. A creature can swim away from the whirlpool only if it first takes an action to pull away and succeeds on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC.
definition.componentsDescription: a mixture of water and dust
